explanation: The LTL property ensures that eventually (F) the autopilot's state becomes TRANSITION (state_is_TRANSITION) when the system is supported and sensor data is good. The use of 'U' means that if the precondition (supported & good) is always false, then the state will never be TRANSITION, which accurately reflects the requirement. If the system is not supported or sensor data is not good, then the transition to TRANSITION state should not happen. Note, the use of X state_is_TRANSITION would not be correct, as the transition might not be immediately. Also the use of G( (supported & good) -> F state_is_TRANSITION) would not be correct, as the implication allows state_is_TRANSITION to occur even when the condition (supported & good) is never true.
